segments.source_text: Identify your key internal customers/stakeholders and assess their business priorities in terms of the 5 rights
How does this information assist the procurement function?

The inputs made by stakeholders (and conflict)
The Roles of Procurement and Supply
In this Unit, we will focus on:

A corporate stakeholder is that which can affect or be affected by the actions of the business as a whole. The stakeholder concept defines stakeholders as “those groups without whose support the organisation would cease to exist”. There are:
From your role or organisations perspective identify examples of  Internal, External and Connected stakeholders.

Take their interests and likely responses into account
Communicate effectively with them on matters that affect them
Engage the interest, support and commitment of influential (and potentially helpful) individuals and groups
Manage potential issues and problem areas that might generate resistance or opposition from influential groups
Stakeholder planning: how to negotiate mutual goal fulfilment?

Utilising Mendelows matrix populate the stakeholder matrix based on their power/interest.
Once completed, consider how you might manage each group?
‘Management’ in terms of communication - Minimal Effort might be progress link on an Intranet

In this Unit, we will focus on:

The central objective will be to encourage support throughout the organisation to achieve the business benefits gained through reducing costs, improving quality, reducing risk, improving timescales etc.
What are the objectives of the function you work in?

The central objective will be to encourage support throughout the organisation to achieve the business benefits gained through reducing costs, improving quality, reducing risk, improving timescales etc.
What are the objectives of the function you work in?

Many functions will seek to enforce policies and procedures through forcing stakeholders to conform to these
Many functions also attempt to create a Gatekeeper role to attempt to control flows of information between stakeholders and external suppliers
Improved results are achieved through engaging with stakeholders to seek commitment to achieve sources of added value - this approach underlines the advisory/consultant role of the function
Consider the links the function has internally and externally
- Where are the internal links? are they positive and collaborative?
- What could you do to understand the external links?

Tutors should note that the syllabus states for this section that  an objective of the function is to link into supply chain analysis/mapping. Tutors should build this into this session to provide linkage to the next session.

In this Unit, we will focus on:

The function can attempt to influence the different tiers of a supply chain.
- How can this be achieved?
The more remote the tier, then generally the more difficult it will be to exert influence.
Why is the case? And what are the potential risks & impact?
In the same way that different internal customers exert conflicting interests on us, the dynamics of a supply chain are even more complicated, so to exert real influence is always difficult.

The Roles of Procurement and Supply
In this Unit, we will focus on:
A key role of procurement can be to influence the organisation’s Triple Bottom Line:

The challenge was that companies should be responsible for three separate bottom lines:
The bottom line measure of corporate profit - “profit account”
The bottom line of a company's “people account”— a measure in some shape or form of how an organisation is socially responsible.
The bottom line of the company's “planet” account” — a measure of how environmentally responsible it has been in its activities.

Assess the policies that are being undertaken in your organisation to pursue the Profit, People & Planet aspects of the triple bottom line
How can you drive value and still maintain the triple bottom line?
How do you drive value through utilising the Triple Bottom Line
Compliance with processes versus the achievement of outcomes
Regulatory requirements that can impact on procedures
Corporate governance applying to procurement and supply
The use of ethical codes in procurement and supply
